Audit these files for places that SILENTLY SWALLOW FAILURES (the anti-pattern where an error/empty/degraded result is hidden so the program proceeds as if it succeeded):

- apps/workers/src/trigger/document-ingestion.ts
- apps/workers/src/trigger/claim-extraction.ts
- apps/workers/src/trigger/claim-extraction-batch-submit.ts
- apps/workers/src/trigger/claim-extraction-batch-drain.ts

For EVERY `catch`, `.catch(...)`, default-on-failure (`?? null/[]/''` after a fallible op), swallowed non-200/404, or "continue/return success after an error" site, classify it as exactly one of:

- **MASKING** — hides a real failure: marks success/complete on failure; silently drops/skips items; inserts degraded data (e.g. null embeddings); swallows a non-OK HTTP status so it looks empty; suppresses logging (esp. prod-only); or otherwise lets the pipeline proceed as if nothing went wrong. Knowledge-corrupting or invisibility bugs count.
- **LEGITIMATE** — genuinely fine to ignore: best-effort temp-file cleanup (`unlink(...).catch`), an idempotency/lock skip that IS recorded/logged, a fatal handler that marks the job/document `failed` and/or rethrows, a documented intentional resilience case.

Output a markdown table: `file:line | one-line what it catches | MASKING or LEGITIMATE | if MASKING: the minimal fix (e.g. "rethrow", "mark failed not complete", "log at error + surface on job", "distinguish 404 from empty")`. Be precise with line numbers. Do NOT edit anything. Be strict but don't flag obviously-correct cleanup. Note: lines already fixed this session — vision model_run logging (~442), extraction-failure now marks `failed` (~1060) — should be classified LEGITIMATE/already-fixed.

90	const CHUNK_SIZE = 4000;
91	// Extraction-window budget: how much chunk text a single extraction call sees.
92	// Meaning lives in CONTEXT that spans chunk boundaries — a multi-paragraph SOP
93	// rule, a debated decision, and (worst case) a diagram whose meaning is entirely
94	// in cross-node arrows. Narrow windows extracted independently fragment that
95	// context, so we keep windows wide. Quote validation is unaffected —
96	// pickEvidenceChunk maps each verbatim quote back to its covering chunk.
97	const MAX_DOCUMENT_TEXT_CHARS = 24_000;
98	// Image transcriptions (especially diagrams/flowcharts) are one connected graph;
99	// give them an even wider window so the whole flow is reasoned over in one call.
100	const MAX_IMAGE_TEXT_CHARS = 32_000;
101	// Vision (Pass 1) sampling. A *thinking* VL model (e.g. qwen3-vl-*-thinking)
102	// spends a reasoning trace BEFORE the transcription text, so the output cap must
103	// be high or it truncates mid-thought (a dense diagram transcription alone is
104	// ~3-4k tokens). Temperature is non-zero on purpose: Qwen recommends AGAINST
105	// greedy decoding for thinking models (temp 0 → repetition loops); 0.6 is their
106	// guidance. Format consistency is enforced by the prompt, not by temperature.
107	const VISION_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS = 32_000;
108	const VISION_TEMPERATURE = 0.6;
109	const VERTEX_FILE_CACHE_MIN_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024;
110	const FALLBACK_ROUTE_ID = 'vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_extraction_primary';

138	// Text helpers
139	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

141	interface TextChunk {
142	  text: string;
143	  /** Inclusive start offset into the source rawText. */
144	  start: number;
145	  /** Exclusive end offset into the source rawText. */
146	  end: number;
147	}
148	
149	type InsertedDocumentChunk = TextChunk & { id: string };
150	
151	/**
152	 * Offsets where a new chunk is ALLOWED to start, in increasing order (always
153	 * includes 0). Boundaries are semantic seams: the start of the line after a
154	 * blank-line gap, and the start of any heading line (`### …`, `#### …`, or a
155	 * numbered section like `2. ### …`). Splitting on these keeps a paragraph, a
156	 * numbered process section, or a swimlane block intact instead of severing it.
157	 */
158	function computeStructuralBoundaries(text: string): number[] {
159	  const set = new Set<number>([0]);
160	  // Alt 1: a blank-line gap → boundary is the start of the following line.
161	  // Alt 2: a newline immediately before a heading line → boundary at the heading.
162	  const re = /\n[ \t]*\n|\n(?=[ \t]*(?:\d+\.\s+)?#{1,6}\s)/g;
163	  let m: RegExpExecArray | null;
164	  while ((m = re.exec(text))) {
165	    set.add(m.index + m[0].length);
166	  }
167	  return [...set].sort((a, b) => a - b);
168	}
169	
170	/**
171	 * Structure-aware chunker. Packs text up to `chunkSize`, but cuts only at
172	 * semantic boundaries (headings / paragraph breaks; then line breaks; only a
173	 * single over-long line forces a hard char cut). Chunks stay contiguous and
174	 * non-overlapping with exact offsets into the source text, so the persisted
175	 * `document_chunks` and `pickEvidenceChunk`'s offset math keep working. This
176	 * replaces the byte-slice fallback that could sever a flowchart arrow line or a
177	 * sentence mid-word.
178	 */
179	function chunkTextStructured(text: string, chunkSize = CHUNK_SIZE): TextChunk[] {
180	  if (text.length <= chunkSize) return [{ text, start: 0, end: text.length }];
181	  const boundaries = computeStructuralBoundaries(text);
182	  const chunks: TextChunk[] = [];
183	  let cursor = 0;
184	  while (cursor < text.length) {
185	    const hardEnd = Math.min(cursor + chunkSize, text.length);
186	    if (hardEnd >= text.length) {
187	      chunks.push({ text: text.slice(cursor, text.length), start: cursor, end: text.length });
188	      break;
189	    }
190	    // Latest structural boundary that fits in (cursor, hardEnd].
191	    let cut = -1;
192	    for (const b of boundaries) {
193	      if (b > cursor && b <= hardEnd) cut = b;
194	      else if (b > hardEnd) break;
195	    }
196	    if (cut === -1) {
197	      // No structural seam fits — fall back to the latest line break, then a
198	      // hard char cut for a single line longer than chunkSize.
199	      const nl = text.slice(cursor, hardEnd).lastIndexOf('\n');
200	      cut = nl > 0 ? cursor + nl + 1 : hardEnd;
201	    }
202	    chunks.push({ text: text.slice(cursor, cut), start: cursor, end: cut });
203	    cursor = cut;
204	  }
205	  return chunks;
206	}
207	
208	function formatDocumentChunksForExtraction(chunks: Array<{ id: string; text: string }>): string {
209	  const parts: string[] = [];
210	  for (const chunk of chunks) {
211	    const header = `DOCUMENT CHUNK\nDocument Chunk ID: ${chunk.id}\nContent:\n`;
212	    const footer = '\n---\n';
213	    parts.push(`${header}${chunk.text}${footer}`);
214	  }
215	  return parts.join('\n');
216	}
217	
218	function buildDocumentChunkWindows<TChunk extends { id: string; text: string }>(
219	  chunks: TChunk[],
220	  maxChars = MAX_DOCUMENT_TEXT_CHARS,
221	): Array<{ chunks: TChunk[]; content: string }> {
222	  const windows: Array<{ chunks: TChunk[]; content: string }> = [];
223	  let current: TChunk[] = [];
224	
225	  for (const chunk of chunks) {
226	    const next = [...current, chunk];
227	    const nextContent = formatDocumentChunksForExtraction(next);
228	    if (current.length > 0 && nextContent.length > maxChars) {
229	      windows.push({ chunks: current, content: formatDocumentChunksForExtraction(current) });
230	      current = [chunk];
231	    } else {
232	      current = next;
233	    }
234	  }
235	
236	  if (current.length > 0) {
237	    windows.push({ chunks: current, content: formatDocumentChunksForExtraction(current) });
238	  }
239	
240	  return windows;
241	}

252	async function extractTextFromXlsx(buffer: Buffer): Promise<string> {
253	  const XLSX = await import('xlsx');
254	  const workbook = XLSX.read(buffer, { type: 'buffer' });
255	  const lines: string[] = [];
256	  for (const sheetName of workbook.SheetNames) {
257	    lines.push(`[Sheet: ${sheetName}]`);
258	    const sheet = workbook.Sheets[sheetName];
259	    if (sheet) {
260	      const csv = XLSX.utils.sheet_to_csv(sheet);
261	      lines.push(csv);
262	    }
263	  }
264	  return lines.join('\n');
265	}

267	async function extractTextFromDocx(buffer: Buffer): Promise<string> {
268	  // mammoth is CommonJS; normalize the namespace whether it comes through as a
269	  // default export or a flat module object.
270	  const mod = (await import('mammoth')) as unknown as {
271	    extractRawText?: (opts: { buffer: Buffer }) => Promise<{ value: string }>;
272	    default?: { extractRawText: (opts: { buffer: Buffer }) => Promise<{ value: string }> };
273	  };
274	  const extractRawText = mod.extractRawText ?? mod.default?.extractRawText;
275	  if (!extractRawText) throw new Error('mammoth.extractRawText unavailable');
276	  const result = await extractRawText({ buffer });
277	  return result.value;
278	}

280	const DOCX_MIME = 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document';

282	const IMAGE_TRANSCRIPTION_SYSTEM = `You are a meticulous visual analyst for an operations knowledge base. You will be shown ONE image an employee uploaded. Produce a faithful, self-contained TEXT rendering of everything the image conveys. Downstream extraction reads ONLY your text — it cannot see the image — and it quotes your text verbatim, so it must be able to reconstruct both the content and the structure from your output alone.
283	
284	1. TRANSCRIBE ALL TEXT VERBATIM
285	Reproduce every piece of visible text exactly as written — labels, headings, every table cell, captions, callouts, stamps, axis labels, legends, handwriting. Never paraphrase, summarize, or normalize wording.
286	
287	2. RENDER STRUCTURE AS A TEXT TOPOLOGY (not free-form prose)
288	When the image contains a diagram, flowchart, org chart, decision tree, map, or schematic, encode its topology so relationships are unambiguous:
289	- Nodes:  [Shape/Color: "verbatim label"]   e.g.  [Blue Diamond: "Credit Check"]
290	- Edges:  CRITICAL — write EACH connection as ONE self-contained line that contains the FULL source node, the arrow (with its label/color/style if any), AND the full target node, e.g.
291	          [Blue Diamond: "Credit Check"] --(Red Arrow: "If Score < 600")--> [Gray Box: "Trigger Manual Deny Email"]
292	  NEVER split a connection across multiple lines, and NEVER put a node on one line and its outgoing arrow/target on the next line. A connection that spans two lines is wrong.
293	- If one node has several outgoing connections, write ONE complete edge line per connection and REPEAT the full source node on each line.
294	- Group swimlanes / columns / sections under headers, e.g.  ### Swimlane: Finance Department
295	Keep the verbatim label text INSIDE the node brackets so it stays exactly quotable. Downstream extraction quotes a WHOLE edge line verbatim to record a handoff, so a complete single-line edge is what lets a relationship become a validated claim — a split edge cannot be quoted and is lost.
296	
297	3. TABLES
298	Render as text preserving rows and columns — one row per line, columns separated consistently, including the header row.
299	
300	4. READING ORDER & GROUPING
301	Present content in natural reading order. Use headers to mark spatial groups, sequence, or hierarchy when they carry meaning.
302	
303	5. OPERATIONAL CONTENT
304	Ensure concrete rules, steps, routing, ownership, dates, quantities, statuses, conditions, and exceptions are explicit.
305	
306	Rules:
307	- Do NOT invent anything that is not visible. If something is unreadable or ambiguous, write [unreadable] or note the ambiguity — never guess.
308	- Output plain text only. You MAY use the simple structural markers above (### headers, the [Node] --(edge)--> [Node] notation, table rows). Do NOT use code fences, and do NOT add commentary or preamble such as "Here is".`;
309	
310	/**
311	 * Build the vision-pass user content with a PROVIDER-NEUTRAL inline image part:
312	 * `{ type:'image', mimeType, data }`. Each adapter translates this to its own
313	 * native shape AT DISPATCH (OpenAI/Qwen/DeepSeek → `image_url`, Anthropic →
314	 * base64 source block, Gemini/Vertex → inlineData).
315	 *
316	 * Why neutral and not provider-shaped here: the route can change between this
317	 * call and actual dispatch (ModelRouter falls back on error). Shaping the image
318	 * for the pre-dispatch provider meant a fallback to a different provider got a
319	 * payload its adapter couldn't read, so the image was silently dropped and the
320	 * model confabulated. Letting the dispatching adapter own the translation makes
321	 * fallback safe.
322	 */
323	function buildVisionMessageContent(
324	  mimeType: string,
325	  base64: string,
326	  requestText: string,
327	): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {
328	  return [
329	    { type: 'text', text: requestText },
330	    { type: 'image', mimeType, data: base64 },
331	  ];
332	}
333	
334	/**
335	 * Pass 1 of image ingestion: send the raw image to the admin-selected
336	 * vision-capable model and get back a faithful text rendering (verbatim text +
337	 * described visual meaning). That text then flows through the SAME chunk →
338	 * extract → validate → promote pipeline as every other document, which keeps
339	 * the quote-validation provenance guarantee intact (claims quote the
340	 * transcription, which is persisted as document_chunks).
341	 *
342	 * The model is chosen in Admin → Settings ("Image vision model") and read here
343	 * via the auxiliary-model registry; the registry's defaultRouteId is only the
344	 * unset/unresolvable fallback.
345	 */
346	async function transcribeImageToText(
347	  client: OracleAIClient,
348	  db: OracleDb,
349	  buffer: Buffer,
350	  doc: { fileName: string; fileType: string; context?: string | null },
351	): Promise<string> {
352	  const fallbackRouteId = VISION_AUXILIARY_MODEL.defaultRouteId;
353	  const route =
354	    (await resolveAuxiliaryRouteFromSettings(db, VISION_AUXILIARY_MODEL.id)) ??
355	    (fallbackRouteId ? getOracleRoute(fallbackRouteId) : null);
356	  if (!route) {
357	    throw new Error(
358	      `vision route unresolvable and default "${fallbackRouteId ?? '(none)'}" is not registered`,
359	    );
360	  }
361	  const mimeType = imageMimeFor(doc.fileType, doc.fileName);
362	  const contextLine =
363	    doc.context && doc.context.trim()
364	      ? ` Context from the uploader about this image: "${doc.context.trim()}". Use it to interpret ambiguous labels, but transcribe only what is actually visible — do not add details that are not in the image.`
365	      : '';
366	  const requestText = `Render the attached image "${doc.fileName}" as faithful, complete text.${contextLine}`;
367	
368	  const blocks = [
369	    makeBlock({
370	      id: 'image-vision-system',
371	      label: 'Image vision transcription system prompt',
372	      kind: 'stable_system',
373	      content: IMAGE_TRANSCRIPTION_SYSTEM,
374	      reasonIncluded: 'vision transcription pass for an uploaded image',
375	    }),
376	    makeBlock({
377	      id: 'image-vision-request',
378	      label: 'Image vision transcription request',
379	      kind: 'dynamic_input',
380	      content: requestText,
381	      reasonIncluded: 'dynamic request paired with the inline image',
382	    }),
383	  ];
384	
385	  const callStartedAt = Date.now();
386	  const result = await client.runText({
387	    taskType: 'document_claim_extraction',
388	    routeId: route.routeId,
389	    promptVersion: EXTRACTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
390	    blocks,
391	    providerOptions: {
392	      // One-shot vision call — nothing reusable to cache.
393	      cache: { disableCache: true },
394	      temperature: VISION_TEMPERATURE,
395	      maxOutputTokens: VISION_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS,
396	      // Keep full image resolution so dense-diagram text stays legible. Qwen-VL
397	      // on DashScope otherwise downscales and the model confabulates; other
398	      // providers ignore this flag (they handle resolution themselves).
399	      highResolutionVision: true,
400	      messages: [
401	        {
402	          role: 'user',
403	          content: buildVisionMessageContent(mimeType, buffer.toString('base64'), requestText),
404	        },
405	      ],
406	    },
407	  });
408	
409	  // The vision pass used to be invisible: nothing recorded WHICH model ran or
410	  // whether the chosen route failed and silently fell back to a different
411	  // provider. That masking is exactly how a broken vision route (e.g. a Qwen
412	  // region/key misconfig) produced a Gemini-fallback transcription that looked
413	  // fine. Record the actual model in `model_runs` and SHOUT on fallback.
414	  const actualProvider = result.provider ?? route.provider;
415	  const actualModel = result.modelId ?? route.modelId;
416	  const fellBack = Boolean(result.fellBackFromRouteId);
417	  if (fellBack) {
418	    console.error(
419	      `[document-ingestion] VISION FALLBACK — the selected vision route "${route.routeId}" ` +
420	        `(${route.provider}/${route.modelId}) FAILED and the call fell back to ` +
421	        `"${result.routeId}" (${actualProvider}/${actualModel}). Reason: ` +
422	        `${result.fallbackReason ?? 'unknown'}. The transcription below was produced by the ` +
423	        `FALLBACK model, NOT the model you selected. Fix the selected route (region/key/model) ` +
424	        `rather than relying on this fallback.`,
425	    );
426	  }
427	  await db
428	    .insert(modelRuns)
429	    .values({
430	      taskType: 'document-ingestion-vision',
431	      model: actualModel,
432	      provider: actualProvider,
433	      promptVersion: EXTRACTION_PROMPT_VERSION,
434	      inputTokens: result.usage?.inputTokens ?? null,
435	      outputTokens: result.usage?.outputTokens ?? null,
436	      latencyMs: Date.now() - callStartedAt,
437	      success: true,
438	      error: fellBack
439	        ? `vision fell back from ${route.routeId} to ${result.routeId}: ${result.fallbackReason ?? 'unknown'}`
440	        : null,
441	    })
442	    .catch((e) => console.error('[document-ingestion] failed to record vision model_run', e));
443	
444	  return result.text ?? '';
445	}

447	/**
448	 * Decide how to parse a document from its declared MIME type, falling back to
449	 * the filename extension when the browser/OS sends a generic
450	 * `application/octet-stream` (common for .docx on some platforms).
451	 */
452	function resolveParseKind(
453	  fileType: string,
454	  fileName: string,
455	): 'pdf' | 'xlsx' | 'docx' | 'image' | 'text' | 'unsupported' {
456	  const ext = fileName.toLowerCase().split('.').pop() ?? '';
457	  if (fileType === 'application/pdf' || ext === 'pdf') return 'pdf';
458	  if (
459	    fileType === 'application/vnd.ms-excel' ||
460	    fileType === 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet' ||
461	    fileType === 'text/csv' ||
462	    ext === 'xlsx' ||
463	    ext === 'xls' ||
464	    ext === 'csv'
465	  ) {
466	    return 'xlsx';
467	  }
468	  if (fileType === DOCX_MIME || ext === 'docx') return 'docx';
469	  // Images go through a vision-model transcription pass. Only the formats
470	  // Gemini accepts natively are routed here; GIF/BMP/TIFF fall through to
471	  // 'unsupported' so we don't hand the model a mime type it rejects.
472	  if (
473	    fileType === 'image/png' ||
474	    fileType === 'image/jpeg' ||
475	    fileType === 'image/jpg' ||
476	    fileType === 'image/webp' ||
477	    fileType === 'image/heic' ||
478	    fileType === 'image/heif' ||
479	    ['png', 'jpg', 'jpeg', 'webp', 'heic', 'heif'].includes(ext)
480	  ) {
481	    return 'image';
482	  }
483	  if (fileType.startsWith('text/') || ['txt', 'md', 'markdown', 'vtt'].includes(ext)) {
484	    return 'text';
485	  }
486	  return 'unsupported';
487	}
488	
489	/**
490	 * Heuristic: does an image transcription look like a diagram (flowchart, swimlane
491	 * map, decision tree) rather than a plain screenshot of prose? The vision prompt
492	 * renders diagrams with `[Lane: "label"]` nodes, `--(Arrow: "…")-->` edges, and
493	 * `### Swimlane` headers, so their presence is a strong signal. Drives a
494	 * relationship-first extraction addendum instead of the box-by-box default.
495	 */
496	function looksLikeDiagramTranscription(text: string): boolean {
497	  return /-->/.test(text) || /--\(Arrow/i.test(text) || /###\s*Swimlane/i.test(text);
498	}
499	
500	/** Map a stored file type / name to a Gemini-accepted image MIME type. */
501	function imageMimeFor(fileType: string, fileName: string): string {
502	  if (fileType.startsWith('image/')) return fileType === 'image/jpg' ? 'image/jpeg' : fileType;
503	  const ext = fileName.toLowerCase().split('.').pop() ?? '';
504	  switch (ext) {
505	    case 'jpg':
506	    case 'jpeg':
507	      return 'image/jpeg';
508	    case 'webp':
509	      return 'image/webp';
510	    case 'heic':
511	      return 'image/heic';
512	    case 'heif':
513	      return 'image/heif';
514	    default:
515	      return 'image/png';
516	  }
517	}

523	interface ProcessDocumentResult {
524	  chunksInserted: number;
525	  candidatesStaged: number;
526	  claimsPromoted: number;
527	  claimsAutoApproved: number;
528	  duplicatesAppended: number;
529	  rejections: number;

532	type ExtractedClaim = ExtractionOutput['claims'][number];
533	
534	type AutoApprovalDecision =
535	  | { approve: true; reason: string }
536	  | { approve: false; reason: string };
537	
538	function hasSensitivityFlags(claim: ExtractedClaim): boolean {
539	  return (
540	    claim.sensitivityFlags?.containsSensitiveHRData === true ||
541	    claim.sensitivityFlags?.containsSensitivePersonalData === true ||
542	    claim.sensitivityFlags?.isPersonalConflict === true
543	  );

546	function decideDocumentClaimAutoApproval(input: {
547	  extracted: ExtractedClaim;
548	  result: ExecutePromotionResult;
549	  quoteVerdict: string;
550	  validTopDomainIds: string[];
551	}): AutoApprovalDecision {
552	  const { extracted, result, quoteVerdict, validTopDomainIds } = input;
553	
554	  if (result.outcome === 'recorded_rejection') {
555	    return { approve: false, reason: 'promotion executor recorded a rejection' };
556	  }
557	  if (!result.claimId) {
558	    return { approve: false, reason: 'promotion executor did not return a claim id' };
559	  }
560	  if (result.stagedEntityProposalIds.length > 0) {
561	    return { approve: false, reason: 'claim staged new entity proposals for human review' };
562	  }
563	  if (!AUTO_APPROVE_CLAIM_TYPES.has(extracted.claimType)) {
564	    return { approve: false, reason: `claim type ${extracted.claimType} requires human review` };
565	  }
566	  if (extracted.requiresReview) {
567	    return { approve: false, reason: 'extractor marked the claim as requiring review' };
568	  }
569	  if (hasSensitivityFlags(extracted)) {
570	    return { approve: false, reason: 'claim has sensitivity flags' };
571	  }
572	  if (extracted.confidenceScore < AUTO_APPROVE_MIN_CONFIDENCE) {
573	    return {
574	      approve: false,
575	      reason: `confidence ${extracted.confidenceScore} is below ${AUTO_APPROVE_MIN_CONFIDENCE}`,
576	    };
577	  }
578	  if (extracted.impactScore > AUTO_APPROVE_MAX_IMPACT) {
579	    return {
580	      approve: false,
581	      reason: `impact ${extracted.impactScore} is above ${AUTO_APPROVE_MAX_IMPACT}`,
582	    };
583	  }
584	  if (!['exact_match', 'normalized_match'].includes(quoteVerdict)) {
585	    return { approve: false, reason: `quote verdict ${quoteVerdict} is not auto-approvable` };
586	  }
587	  if (validTopDomainIds.length === 0) {
588	    return { approve: false, reason: 'no valid top-domain assignments' };
589	  }
590	
591	  return {
592	    approve: true,
593	    reason:
594	      'validated document claim met conservative auto-approval policy: high confidence, low/medium impact, supported quote, valid taxonomy, no sensitivity flags, no entity proposals',
595	  };
596	}

598	async function autoApproveDocumentClaimIfEligible(input: {
599	  db: OracleDb;
600	  candidateId: string;
601	  extracted: ExtractedClaim;
602	  result: ExecutePromotionResult;
603	  quoteVerdict: string;
604	  validTopDomainIds: string[];
605	}): Promise<boolean> {
606	  const { db, candidateId, extracted, result, quoteVerdict, validTopDomainIds } = input;
607	  const decision = decideDocumentClaimAutoApproval({
608	    extracted,
609	    result,
610	    quoteVerdict,
611	    validTopDomainIds,
612	  });
613	  const metadataJson = {
614	    policy: 'document_claim_auto_approval_v1',
615	    decision: decision.approve ? 'approve' : 'skip',
616	    reason: decision.reason,
617	    outcome: result.outcome,
618	    claimId: result.claimId ?? null,
619	    claimType: extracted.claimType,
620	    confidenceScore: extracted.confidenceScore,
621	    impactScore: extracted.impactScore,
622	    requiresReview: extracted.requiresReview,
623	    quoteVerdict,
624	    validTopDomainIds,
625	    stagedEntityProposalIds: result.stagedEntityProposalIds,
626	  };
627	
628	  if (!decision.approve || !result.claimId) {
629	    await db.insert(extractionValidationResults).values({
630	      candidateId,
631	      checkName: 'promotion_transaction',
632	      status: 'skipped',
633	      detail: `Auto-approval skipped: ${decision.reason}.`,
634	      metadataJson,
635	    });
636	    return false;
637	  }
638	
639	  const [currentClaim] = await db
640	    .select({ id: claims.id, status: claims.status })
641	    .from(claims)
642	    .where(eq(claims.id, result.claimId))
643	    .limit(1);
644	
645	  if (!currentClaim) {
646	    await db.insert(extractionValidationResults).values({
647	      candidateId,
648	      checkName: 'promotion_transaction',
649	      status: 'skipped',
650	      detail: `Auto-approval skipped: claim ${result.claimId} was not found after promotion.`,
651	      metadataJson,
652	    });
653	    return false;
654	  }
655	
656	  if (currentClaim.status !== 'pending_review') {
657	    await db.insert(extractionValidationResults).values({
658	      candidateId,
659	      checkName: 'promotion_transaction',
660	      status: 'skipped',
661	      detail: `Auto-approval skipped: claim ${result.claimId} is already ${currentClaim.status}.`,
662	      metadataJson,
663	    });
664	    return false;
665	  }
666	
667	  await db
668	    .update(claims)
669	    .set({ status: 'approved' })
670	    .where(and(eq(claims.id, result.claimId), eq(claims.status, 'pending_review')));
671	  await db.insert(extractionValidationResults).values({
672	    candidateId,
673	    checkName: 'promotion_transaction',
674	    status: 'pass',
675	    detail: `Auto-approved claim ${result.claimId}: ${decision.reason}.`,
676	    metadataJson,
677	  });
678	
679	  return true;
680	}

682	async function processDocument(
683	  documentId: string,
684	  jobRunId: string,
685	): Promise<ProcessDocumentResult> {
686	  const db = getDirectDb();
687	  const client = buildOracleClient();
688	  const serviceSupabase = createServiceRoleClient();
689	  const outcome: ProcessDocumentResult = {
690	    chunksInserted: 0,
691	    candidatesStaged: 0,
692	    claimsPromoted: 0,
693	    claimsAutoApproved: 0,
694	    duplicatesAppended: 0,
695	    rejections: 0,
696	  };
697	
698	  // 1. Load + mark processing.
699	  const [doc] = await db.select().from(documents).where(eq(documents.id, documentId)).limit(1);
700	  if (!doc) throw new Error(`Document ${documentId} not found`);
701	  if (doc.status === 'complete') {
702	    console.log(`[document-ingestion] ${documentId} already complete — skipping`);
703	    return outcome;
704	  }
705	  await db
706	    .update(documents)
707	    .set({ status: 'processing', processingError: null, processedAt: null })
708	    .where(eq(documents.id, documentId));
709	
710	  const parseKind = resolveParseKind(doc.fileType, doc.fileName);
711	  const route = await resolveExtractionRoute(db);
712	  const activeTopDomainIds = await loadActiveTopDomainIds(db);
713	  const topDomainNameMap = await loadTopDomainNameMap(db);
714	  const entityRegistry = await loadEntityRegistry(db);
715	  const correctionLessons = await loadClaimCorrectionLessonPack(db);
716	
717	  // 2. Download.
718	  const { data: blob, error: downloadError } = await serviceSupabase.storage
719	    .from(doc.storageBucket)
720	    .download(doc.storagePath);
721	  if (downloadError || !blob) {
722	    throw new Error(
723	      `Storage download failed for ${doc.storagePath}: ${downloadError?.message ?? 'empty blob'}`,
724	    );
725	  }
726	  const buffer = Buffer.from(await blob.arrayBuffer());
727	  // The file-backed Vertex cache feeds the raw artifact to the EXTRACTION pass.
728	  // For images that pass runs over the vision transcription (text), not the
729	  // image, so never attach the raw image as a file-backed cache source.
730	  const fileBackedCachePath =
731	    route.provider === 'vertex' &&
732	    parseKind !== 'image' &&
733	    buffer.length > VERTEX_FILE_CACHE_MIN_BYTES
734	      ? await materializeVertexCacheTempFile(buffer, doc.fileName)
735	      : null;
736	
737	  // 3. Parse.
738	  let rawText: string;
739	  try {
740	    if (parseKind === 'pdf') {
741	      rawText = await extractTextFromPdf(buffer);
742	    } else if (parseKind === 'xlsx') {
743	      rawText = await extractTextFromXlsx(buffer);
744	    } else if (parseKind === 'docx') {
745	      rawText = await extractTextFromDocx(buffer);
746	    } else if (parseKind === 'image') {
747	      // Pass 1: vision model renders the image to faithful text. Prefix a
748	      // provenance header so the persisted chunk is self-describing.
749	      const transcription = await transcribeImageToText(client, db, buffer, doc);
750	      rawText = transcription.trim()
751	        ? `Visual transcription of uploaded image "${doc.fileName}" (${doc.fileType}), produced by a vision model because the source is an image rather than text.\n\n${transcription}`
752	        : '';
753	    } else if (parseKind === 'text') {
754	      rawText = buffer.toString('utf-8');
755	    } else {
756	      console.warn(
757	        `[document-ingestion] unsupported file type ${doc.fileType} for doc ${documentId} — marking complete with note`,
758	      );
759	      await db
760	        .update(documents)
761	        .set({
762	          status: 'complete',
763	          processedAt: new Date(),
764	          processingError: `Unsupported file type: ${doc.fileType}`,
765	        })
766	        .where(eq(documents.id, documentId));
767	      if (fileBackedCachePath) {
768	        await unlink(fileBackedCachePath).catch(() => undefined);
769	      }
770	      return outcome;
771	    }
772	  } catch (parseErr) {
773	    if (fileBackedCachePath) {
774	      await unlink(fileBackedCachePath).catch(() => undefined);
775	    }
776	    throw new Error(
777	      `File parse failed for ${doc.fileName}: ${parseErr instanceof Error ? parseErr.message : String(parseErr)}`,
778	    );
779	  }
780	
781	  if (!rawText.trim()) {
782	    await db
783	      .update(documents)
784	      .set({ status: 'complete', processedAt: new Date(), processingError: 'No text extracted' })
785	      .where(eq(documents.id, documentId));
786	    if (fileBackedCachePath) {
787	      await unlink(fileBackedCachePath).catch(() => undefined);
788	    }
789	    return outcome;
790	  }
791	
792	  // 4. Chunk + embed + insert chunks (idempotent dedup by content hash).
793	  //    Structure-aware: cut on headings/paragraphs/lines, never mid-line, so a
794	  //    flowchart arrow line or a sentence is never severed across chunks.
795	  const textChunks = chunkTextStructured(rawText);
796	  let chunkVectors: (number[] | null)[] = [];
797	  try {
798	    const { vectors } = await embedMany(textChunks.map((c) => c.text));
799	    chunkVectors = vectors;
800	  } catch (embedErr) {
801	    console.warn('[document-ingestion] embed failed; inserting chunks without vectors', embedErr);
802	    chunkVectors = textChunks.map(() => null);
803	  }
804	
805	  const insertedChunkIds: InsertedDocumentChunk[] = [];
806	  for (let i = 0; i < textChunks.length; i++) {
807	    const chunkTextWindow = textChunks[i]!;
808	    const contentHash = createHash('sha256').update(chunkTextWindow.text).digest('hex');
809	    try {
810	      const [chunk] = await db
811	        .insert(documentChunks)
812	        .values({
813	          documentId,
814	          chunkIndex: i,
815	          rawText: chunkTextWindow.text,
816	          tokenCount: Math.ceil(chunkTextWindow.text.length / 4),
817	          contentHash,
818	          embedding: chunkVectors[i] ?? null,
819	          metadataJson: { fileType: doc.fileType, fileName: doc.fileName },
820	        })
821	        .onConflictDoNothing()
822	        .returning({ id: documentChunks.id });
823	      if (chunk) {
824	        outcome.chunksInserted += 1;
825	        insertedChunkIds.push({ id: chunk.id, ...chunkTextWindow });
826	      } else {
827	        const [existingChunk] = await db
828	          .select({ id: documentChunks.id })
829	          .from(documentChunks)
830	          .where(
831	            and(
832	              eq(documentChunks.documentId, documentId),
833	              eq(documentChunks.contentHash, contentHash),
834	            ),
835	          )
836	          .limit(1);
837	        if (existingChunk) {
838	          insertedChunkIds.push({ id: existingChunk.id, ...chunkTextWindow });
839	        }
840	      }
841	    } catch (chunkErr) {
842	      console.error(`[document-ingestion] chunk ${i} insert failed:`, chunkErr);
843	    }
844	  }
845	
846	  if (insertedChunkIds.length === 0 || rawText.trim().length <= 20) {
847	    await db
848	      .update(documents)
849	      .set({ status: 'complete', processedAt: new Date() })
850	      .where(eq(documents.id, documentId));
851	    if (fileBackedCachePath) {
852	      await unlink(fileBackedCachePath).catch(() => undefined);
853	    }
854	    return outcome;
855	  }
856	
857	  // 5. Process every chunk window. Each model call is bounded, but the
858	  // document itself is not silently truncated.
859	  const extractionWindows = buildDocumentChunkWindows(
860	    insertedChunkIds,
861	    parseKind === 'image' ? MAX_IMAGE_TEXT_CHARS : MAX_DOCUMENT_TEXT_CHARS,
862	  );
863	  for (let windowIndex = 0; windowIndex < extractionWindows.length; windowIndex++) {
864	    const extractionWindow = extractionWindows[windowIndex]!;
865	    const windowChunks = extractionWindow.chunks;
866	    const documentCorpus = extractionWindow.content;
867	    const sourceHash = createHash('sha256').update(documentCorpus, 'utf8').digest('hex');
868	    const [batch] = await db
869	      .insert(extractionBatches)
870	      .values({
871	        jobRunId,
872	        batchType: 'document_page',
873	        status: 'pending_model',
874	        sourceDocumentChunkIds: windowChunks.map((c) => c.id),
875	        sourceHash,
876	        modelRunIdsAttempted: [],
877	        routeIdsAttempted: [route.routeId],
878	      })
879	      .returning({ id: extractionBatches.id });
880	    if (!batch) throw new Error('[document-ingestion] failed to insert extraction_batches row');
881	
882	    // 6. Compile prompt + context pack. The document text becomes the dynamic
883	    //    block; the extraction system prompt + the document-specific addendum
884	    //    are stable.
885	    const isDiagram = parseKind === 'image' && looksLikeDiagramTranscription(rawText);
886	    const baseDocumentNote =
887	      parseKind === 'image'
888	        ? isDiagram
889	          ? `\n\nNOTE: The text below is a VISION-MODEL TRANSCRIPTION of an uploaded DIAGRAM (flowchart / swimlane / process map). Nodes are written as [Lane/Color: "label"] and connections as [A] --(Arrow: "condition")--> [B]. The MEANING of this document is the FLOW — who hands off to whom, in what sequence, and under which conditions — NOT the existence of individual boxes.\nImage name: ${doc.fileName}\nFile type: ${doc.fileType}\n\nDIAGRAM EXTRACTION GUIDANCE:\n- Prefer claims that capture HANDOFFS and SEQUENCE. Represent each arrow as a dependency claim — "After/from X, Y happens" or "X is handed off to <role/lane> who does Y" — and include the arrow's condition label when one is present.\n- Capture DECISION/BRANCH rules (e.g. "If Audit: Fail", "Existing Product" vs "New Product Type", "Before an Order") as exception or process rules that state the condition AND the resulting path.\n- Record OWNERSHIP only when it adds information (which lane/role performs a step). Do NOT emit a separate claim for every box: a bare node label with no relationship is low-value — fold it into the handoff claim it participates in.\n- Aim for fewer, higher-altitude, CONNECTED claims rather than many disconnected "box X exists" statements.\n- Every exactQuote must still be copied verbatim from within ONE provided document chunk. For a handoff, quote the full \`[A] --(Arrow: "…")--> [B]\` line; for a branch, quote the line that contains the condition.`
890	          : `\n\nNOTE: The text below is a VISION-MODEL TRANSCRIPTION of an uploaded image (not a conversation, not a native text document). Extract claims about operational processes, rules, systems, and dependencies that are explicitly supported by this transcription.\nImage name: ${doc.fileName}\nFile type: ${doc.fileType}`

The catch swallows only a logging side-effect, not the fact itself. |
| 768, 774, 787, 852, 1073, 1392 | `.catch(() => undefined)` on temp file `unlink()` | **LEGITIMATE** | Best-effort cleanup. File deletion is idempotent; failure doesn't corrupt anything. Appropriate for one-shot resource cleanup. |

Insert flag into `documentChunks.metadataJson` noting degraded state so retrieval/analytics can filter. |
| 841–843 | Chunk insert wrapped in try-catch; on error: `console.error` then continue loop | **MASKING** | The error is logged but the document status is never marked `failed`. If all chunks fail to insert, `insertedChunkIds.length === 0` exits early (846), but partial failures silently skip those chunks. Persist the error detail to a `document_chunk_errors` audit table or at minimum set `document.processingError` with a summary + count. |
| 1040–1076 | Model extraction call wrapped in try-catch; on error: marks batch `failed` + document `failed` + returns outcome | **LEGITIMATE/ALREADY-FIXED** | Correct. Error is surfaced: batch gets `status='failed'` with the error message, document gets `status='failed'`, and the job returns early. This is NOT silent. |
| 1122 | `if (!candidate) continue;` after candidate insert | **MASKING** | The candidate row returned null (insert succeeded but returning() returned empty). This is rare but silent — no validation result is recorded to explain why this candidate was skipped. Log at warn level + insert a `validation_result` row with `status='failed', detail='candidate insert returned no row'`. |

The lock-busy path correctly skips without rejecting (the candidate stays `validated` for next cron). |
| **claim-extraction.ts** | | | |
| 249–254 | Segment processing wrapped in try-catch; on error: increment totals.errors + log, but **continue loop** | **MASKING** | The segment error path in processSegment already handles it (marks batch + messages `failed`), but the catch here suppresses that error from propagating. However, it's caught and logged. The issue: if segment processing partially succeeds (some candidates promoted, then a late error), the partial success isn't rolled back. The design accepts this. **LEGITIMATE IF INTENDED** — document that late errors are non-fatal and messages stay marked `complete`. |
